How the Tray Ad Builder works
Image guidelines
Top — 32px
Badge pill floats here. Keep top-left corner clear of heavy graphics.
Creative — 76px
Full-bleed image. Min 259×146 at 16:9. No text overlays here.
Bottom — 40px
Dark gradient auto-applied. Logo + copy (max 70 chars) + arrow.
How tray assignment works
You enterBrand name + slogan + optional description
→
Keywords scoredEvery word matched against each tray's keyword bank
→
Highest score winsOnly trays scoring above zero are shown
Keyword scoring weights
Slogan match Exact phrase from your slogan field matches a tray's slogan bank +15 pts
Primary keyword Core identity nouns — category names, key emotions, product types +10 pts
Supporting keyword Related words — occasions, activities, products, cultural signals +8 pts
Example — Cadbury
YOU ENTER
Brand: Cadbury
Slogan: Make something good happen
SCORES
Romance: +15 (slogan match)
+ +8 (chocolate)
All others: 0
RESULT
💕 Romance Shows
Only this tray scores above zero — auto-assigned

If nothing matches, add more context in the description — product category, occasion, or emotion words. You can always switch to an alternate tray using the chips shown after assignment.

The 10 content trays
TrayBest for brands that signal…Example keywords
Romance ShowsLove, gifting, togethernesschocolate, jewelry, valentine, anniversary
Comedy CarnivalFun, irreverence, youth culturesnacks, cola, gaming, yolo, dhamaal
Popular in ActionPower, speed, performancebike, tyre, gym, energy drink, toofan
Travel MoviesExploration, freedom, journeyairline, hotel, travel more, wanderlust
Popular Near YouLocal, quick, conveniencedelivery, grocery, UPI, near you, order
Binge DramaEmotional depth, aspiration, dramafeel every moment, obsession, story, intense
Great Indian CinemaHeritage, pride, authentic Indiadesh, ghar, har ghar, made in india
Family / KidsCare, safety, nurturedetergent, baby, daag, trusted by mothers
Feels Like HomeHome décor, appliances, livingfurniture, paint, renovation, ghar
For Good TimesCelebration, achievement, joybirthday, premium, toast, jashan, diwali
The badge framework — "Why am I seeing this?"

The badge answers one question for the viewer: "Why is this ad appearing in front of me right now?" Not what the brand is. Not what the offer is. Why now, why here, why you. It sits at the intersection of the content tray the viewer is in and the cultural moment happening right now.

Evergreen badges
Always available. Fire whenever your brand matches the tray, regardless of date. Answers: why this tray?
GIFT YOUR PARTNER
PUSH YOUR LIMITS
FOR YOUR HOME
Seasonal / Moment badges
Time-windowed. Override evergreen during festivals, seasons, events. Answers: why right now?
THIS VALENTINE'S
FOR SUMMER TRIPS
CELEBRATE DIWALI NOW
Three rules every badge must pass
1
Second person. Speak to the viewer directly. "YOUR TRIP AWAITS" not "TRAVEL BRAND AD". The viewer should feel spoken to, not described at.
2
Present tense. What is happening now, as they watch — not what happened or will happen. "THIS VALENTINE'S" not "VALENTINE'S DAY SALE COMING SOON".
3
Three words max. A badge is a signal, not a sentence. Every word must name the moment, the person, or the action. If it does none of those three things, cut it.
✗ Brand-echo (weaker)
MADE FOR LOVE
DAAG ACCHE HAIN
Describes the brand. Viewer feels observed, not addressed.
✓ Viewer-addressed (stronger)
THIS VALENTINE'S
YOUR DESI PICK
Mirrors the viewer's current moment. Feels personal, not promotional.

Brand details
Name + slogan drive tray assignment via keyword logic.
Auto-assigned tray
Assigned by matching slogan keywords to tray emotional signals. Switch if needed.
Creative image
Fills the full ad behind the overlays. Badge colour is extracted from it.
📐 Guidelines
  • Minimum 259 × 146 px · 16:9 recommended
  • Larger images auto-resized
  • Non-16:9 can be cropped to fit
  • No heavy text — copy goes in the strip below

  • Transparent PNG ideal
  • Rounded corners applied automatically
